diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Partially digested blood in vomit localises the source to the upper GI tract.

symptoms.symptom_name: Melaena — two episodes of black, tarry, foul-smelling stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms a significant volume of blood transiting the gastrointestinal tract.
Postural symptoms indicate significant intravascular volume depletion with compensatory failure on standing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy alcohol use and occasional NSAID use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Both alcohol and NSAID use are recognised causes of gastric mucosal injury and peptic ulceration predisposing to upper GI bleeding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension, tachycardia, and delayed capillary refill on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective signs of hypovolaemic shock; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Actively bleeding mucosal lesion or visible vessel on upper endoscopy

investigation_details.expected_result: Actively bleeding mucosal lesion or visible vessel, most likely peptic ulcer or gastritis given the alcohol/NSAID history
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Bleeding source and haemostasis unconfirmed; telehealth cannot perform this and should recommend it occur urgently in hospital.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive diagnostic and therapeutic procedure for upper GI bleeding.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute upper gastrointestinal bleeding with haemodynamic compromise
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Coffee-ground haematemesis, witnessed melaena, and postural presyncope together indicate significant active upper GI haemorrhage with early haemodynamic compensation failure, mandating immediate emergency ambulance dispatch.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, coagulation studies, group and crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses degree of anaemia and prepares for possible transfusion; guides resuscitation.
Definitive diagnostic and therapeutic procedure to identify and treat the bleeding source; cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient stool occult blood testing as first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Grossly inappropriate given overt haematemesis, melaena, and presyncope — this requires immediate emergency assessment, not deferred outpatient testing.
